title: Re-initializing an `AtomicDict` in threads segfaults
author: devdanzin
body:
This is a bug that was [rejected as invalid in CPython](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/127192#issuecomment-2495552818): supporting re-initializing objects in different threads is not planned. I only report it here, hoping it can be of help somehow, because fusil stumbled upon this bug again.


The following code causes many different segfaults and at least one abort:

```python
from threading import Thread
from time import sleep
import cereggii
a = cereggii.AtomicDict()

def f():
    for x in range(100):
        a.__init__()  # This is cheating!
        sleep(0.005)
        for y in range(10):
            sleep(0.0005)
            a[y * x] = x

alive = [Thread(target=f, args=()) for _ in range(100)]

for x in alive:
    x.start()
```

IIRC, the issue is that threads will see a half-initialized object.

comments.author: dpdani
comments.body:
Ok, I see Sam's reasoning for the issue in CPython, that ensuring only one thread can run the init function is hard to do in general. (I'm thinking it would probably require modifications to the object headers.)

But I think this can be done pretty easily for AtomicDict, since the internal state that gets initialized inside `__init__` is handled through an [`AtomicRef`](https://dpdani.github.io/cereggii/api/AtomicRef/).

In practice, [this debugging assert](https://dpdani.github.io/cereggii/api/AtomicRef/) can be turned into a stable check, and the write can be done atomically to ensure that only one thread can run the init function.
